title: rhus toxicodendron subsp. radicans
intro: Rhus toxicodendron subsp. radicans, also known as poison ivy, is a plant species native to North America that contains an oily sap called urushiol, causing severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in most people. Traditionally, poison ivy has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been utilized in various herbal preparations. Some of the health benefits attributed to poison ivy include wound healing, antiviral properties, and potential relief from arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Herbal preparations of poison ivy, such as ointments, salves, and tinctures, are often used externally to treat skin conditions, although their use should be approached with caution due to the potential for severe allergic reactions.
